name: Metropolitan Teachers CU
description_short: Nashville-based credit union serving Metro Nashville educators since the 1950s, offering savings accounts, auto loans, and home repair loans at competitive rates.
description_long:
Metropolitan Teachers Credit Union (MTCU) was founded in the 1950s by eight educators who banded together to address the financial challenges facing African American teachers in Nashville. Operating under a State Charter, the institution grew steadily under the leadership of figures including Joseph J. Officer, Joseph Martin, and Dr. Eunice P. Grisby, eventually reaching million-dollar status and evolving into the full-service credit union it is today. Current CEO Samuel Kirk continues that mission, focusing on growth and competitive financial services for members.

MTCU offers a focused suite of financial products tailored to its membership: savings accounts, auto loans starting as low as 5.00% APR, summer personal loans starting at 12.00% APR, and home repair loans (rates available by phone). Members also gain access to online account enrollment, a digital account portal, and financial calculators through the website. The credit union's routing number (264080808) confirms it operates as a full chartered depository institution.

What distinguishes MTCU is its deep community roots and mission-driven origin. It was explicitly created to serve African American teachers who were excluded from mainstream financial institutions in the Jim Crow era — a legacy that gives the institution a distinct identity among Nashville credit unions. Membership is open to employees of the Metropolitan Nashville Board of Education and their immediate family, as well as small businesses and their employees located within 25 miles of the credit union, broadening access beyond just educators.

Honestly assessed, MTCU is a small, community-focused institution with a narrow product set and very limited operating hours (2:00–5:00 PM weekdays only). The featured rates on the website were last updated September 2023, which may not reflect current terms. There is no mention of checking accounts, debit cards, or ATM network access — prospective members should call ahead to confirm what day-to-day banking services are available. For educators and school staff in Nashville who value community mission and lower loan rates over full-service banking convenience, MTCU is a solid, trusted option. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Auto loans available as low as 5.00% APR — competitive rate for members
Summer personal loans starting at 12.00% APR — useful for seasonal expenses
Home repair loan product specifically available for property improvements
Online account enrollment and digital account portal available
70+ year institutional history with deep roots in Nashville's educator community
Membership extended to small businesses within 25-mile radius, not just teachers
Established routing number (264080808) confirms full chartered depository status
cons: Extremely limited hours: Monday–Friday, 2:00–5:00 PM only (3 hours per day)
Narrow membership field — primarily Metro Nashville Board of Education employees and family
Featured rates on website are dated September 2023 — may not be current
No mention of checking accounts, debit cards, or ATM network access
Single branch location on Jefferson St — no additional offices
best_for: Metro Nashville public school teachers and MNPS district employees
Immediate family members of Nashville Board of Education staff seeking credit union rates
Nashville-area small business owners within 25 miles wanting access to a community credit union
Members seeking lower-rate auto or home improvement loans with a mission-driven institution
diagnosis: Metropolitan Teachers Credit Union is best suited for Metro Nashville Board of Education employees, their families, and nearby small business owners who want community-rooted banking with competitive loan rates. The main caveat is severely limited operating hours (2–5 PM weekdays only) and a narrow product set — prospective members should call to confirm whether checking accounts or ATM access are available before switching primary banking.
services: Credit union membership for educators and small businesses
meta_description: Nashville credit union serving Metro Nashville educators since the 1950s, offering savings accounts, auto loans, and home repair loans.
